goal: Promote the `search_code` MCP tool from a `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub to a real handler that merges (a) tree-sitter symbol-index matches from the SQLite knowledge graph and (b) ripgrep-style text matches scanned in-process via the `ignore` + `grep-searcher` + `grep-regex` Rust libraries under a caller-supplied filesystem root, de-duplicating by `(file_path, line_number)` — satisfying feature P1-W5-F09 and master-plan §3.2 row 4 / §18 Phase 1 Week 5 line 1765.
plan_summary: Add a new `KnowledgeGraph::search_entities_by_name(&self, query: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` read helper to `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` that returns entities whose `name` OR `qualified_name` matches the caller query via a parameterised `LIKE` scan, ordered by `t_ingested_at DESC` and capped at `limit`. Add a new `crates/ucil-daemon/src/text_search.rs` module wrapping `ignore::WalkBuilder` + `grep-searcher::Searcher` + `grep-regex::RegexMatcherBuilder` into `fn text_search(root: &Path, query: &str, max_results: usize) -> Result<Vec<TextMatch>, TextSearchError>` that walks `root` (respecting `.gitignore` / hidden-file rules), streams per-file matches via `Searcher::search_path`, and returns `{file_path, line_number, line_text}` records up to the cap. Wire a new `handle_search_code(id, params, kg)` branch in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::McpServer::handle_tools_call` mirroring the `find_definition` / `get_conventions` KG-gated dispatch pattern (WO-0033 / WO-0034). The handler extracts `arguments.query` (required string) and optional `arguments.root` (string → `PathBuf`) and `arguments.max_results` (integer, default 50, hard cap 500), then runs the symbol search under a KG mutex and the text search on the tokio blocking pool via `spawn_blocking`, merges by `(file_path, line_number)` preferring the symbol match when a tie occurs, and returns the MCP `tools/call` envelope shape `result: {_meta: {tool: "search_code", source: "tree-sitter+ripgrep", count, query, root, symbol_match_count, text_match_count, results: [...]}, content: [{type: "text", text: "..."}], isError: false}`. Each result carries `{source: "symbol" | "text" | "both", file_path, line_number, preview, qualified_name?, signature?}`. Missing `root` defaults to the current working directory (`std::env::current_dir`). Write `crates/ucil-daemon/src/text_search.rs` + a `#[cfg(test)]` unit test validating walker + matcher behaviour on a real `tempfile::TempDir` tree. Write the frozen acceptance test `server::test_search_code_basic` as a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` item in `server.rs` (same scope as `test_find_definition_tool` / `test_get_conventions_tool`) that creates a temp project with a real Rust source file, inserts a matching `Entity` into a temp KG via `upsert_entity`, builds `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph`, calls `handle_line` with a `tools/call` request for `search_code`, and asserts the merged response contains both the symbol match and the text match de-duplicated correctly. Every WO-0034 scope-out line continues to apply — no edits to `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`, `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json`, or the master plan.
scope_in: New workspace dependencies in root `Cargo.toml` under `[workspace.dependencies]`: `ignore = "0.4"`, `grep-searcher = "0.1"`, `grep-regex = "0.1"`, `grep-matcher = "0.1"`. Document each with a one-paragraph rustdoc-style comment block mirroring the existing workspace dep conventions (e.g. the `notify = "6"` block lines 53-61) citing `DEC-0009-search-code-in-process-ripgrep` and feature `P1-W5-F09`.

New public read helper `KnowledgeGraph::search_entities_by_name(&self, query: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` in `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs`. SQL: `SELECT id, qualified_name, name, entity_type, file_path, start_line, end_line, signature, doc_comment, parent_id, branch_scope, source_project, vendor_marker, confidence, t_valid_from, t_valid_to, t_ingested_at FROM entities WHERE name LIKE ?1 OR qualified_name LIKE ?1 ORDER BY t_ingested_at DESC LIMIT ?2`. Query parameter is wrapped as `%query%` by the helper; `limit` is bound as-is. Returns `Vec<Entity>`; never `Err` on empty set. `#[tracing::instrument]` span `ucil.core.kg.search_entities_by_name` with field `query_len=query.len()` and `limit`. Rustdoc refs master-plan §12.1 (entities schema) and feature `P1-W5-F09`.

Unit tests under `#[cfg(test)]` in `knowledge_graph.rs` covering: (a) exact-name match — insert two entities with different names, query one, assert only the match returns; (b) substring match — insert `foo_bar`, query `foo`, assert match; (c) qualified_name match — insert `crate::module::foo`, query `module::foo`, assert match; (d) limit respected — insert 5 matching entities, query with limit=3, assert exactly 3 returned; (e) ordering — insert two matching entities with observable `t_ingested_at` difference, assert the newer one is first; (f) empty-table — fresh KG, query anything, returns `Ok(vec![])`. All tests use `tempfile::TempDir` + `KnowledgeGraph::open`; no mocks.

New `crates/ucil-daemon/src/text_search.rs` module (declared in `lib.rs` as `pub(crate) mod text_search;`). Public items: `pub(crate) struct TextMatch { pub file_path: PathBuf, pub line_number: u64, pub line_text: String }` (derive Debug+Clone+PartialEq), `pub(crate) enum TextSearchError` (`#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)]` with variants `BuildMatcher(#[from] grep_regex::Error)`, `Io(#[from] std::io::Error)`, `Walk(String)`), and `pub(crate) fn text_search(root: &Path, query: &str, max_results: usize) -> Result<Vec<TextMatch>, TextSearchError>`. Implementation: build a `grep_regex::RegexMatcherBuilder::new().case_smart(true).build(query)?` matcher; construct an `ignore::WalkBuilder::new(root)` with defaults (respects `.gitignore`, hidden-file exclusion, symlink loop break); iterate entries, skip directories + non-UTF8 paths, for each file call `grep_searcher::SearcherBuilder::new().line_number(true).build().search_path(&matcher, entry.path(), UserSink { ... })` where `UserSink` implements `grep_searcher::Sink` to push `TextMatch` rows into an owned `Vec` and early-terminate (`break` by returning `SinkFinishError`) once the cap is reached. Bound memory via `grep_searcher`'s line iterator; skip binary files (`SearcherBuilder::binary_detection(BinaryDetection::quit(b'\0'))`).

A `#[cfg(test)]` unit test in `text_search.rs` (`mod tests { ... }`) that: (a) creates a `tempfile::TempDir`, writes `a.rs` containing `fn hello_world() { /* marker */ }` and `b.txt` containing two lines `foo hello_world bar\nanother line`, and a `.gitignore` entry for `skip.log` plus a matching `skip.log` file with `hello_world` text; (b) calls `text_search(tmp.path(), "hello_world", 50)`; (c) asserts the result contains entries for `a.rs` and `b.txt` but NOT `skip.log` (proves `.gitignore` is respected); (d) asserts `line_number` is 1-indexed and matches the actual file position; (e) asserts `max_results=1` caps the result set.

Extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::McpServer::handle_tools_call` with a third KG-gated dispatch branch: when `name == "search_code" && self.kg.is_some()`, call `Self::handle_search_code(id, params, self.kg.as_ref().unwrap())`. The existing `find_definition` and `get_conventions` branches are untouched. When `name == "search_code"` but no KG is attached, fall through to the stub; phase-1 invariant #9 preserved for every other tool.

New `handle_search_code(id: &Value, params: &Value, kg: &Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>) -> Value` method in `server.rs` that: (1) extracts required `arguments.query` as `&str` — missing / non-string → JSON-RPC error `-32602`; reject empty string as `-32602` (`search_code: arguments.query must be non-empty`); (2) extracts optional `arguments.root` as `Option<&str>` (missing/null = no filter → defaults to `std::env::current_dir`); rejects non-string `root` as `-32602`; rejects `root` that does not exist or is not a directory as `-32602` with a descriptive message; (3) extracts optional `arguments.max_results` as `Option<i64>` (missing/null = 50); rejects non-integer / negative / zero as `-32602`; caps to a const `SEARCH_CODE_MAX_RESULTS: usize = 500` (values above 500 are clamped down, logged via `tracing::warn`); (4) acquires the KG mutex, calls `search_entities_by_name(query, max_results)` capped at `max_results`, releases the mutex before text search; (5) runs the text search on the blocking pool via `tokio::task::spawn_blocking({ let root = root.clone(); let query = query.to_owned(); move || text_search(&root, &query, max_results) })` and `.await`s the join handle; (6) merges symbol + text results — dedup key is `(file_path, line_number)`; when both sources hit the same key the merged record carries `source: "both"` and keeps the symbol's `qualified_name` + `signature`; (7) caps the merged list at `max_results`; (8) projects each merged row to a JSON object `{source, file_path, line_number, preview, qualified_name?, signature?}`; (9) returns the structured `tools/call` envelope with `_meta: {tool: "search_code", source: "tree-sitter+ripgrep", count, query, root, symbol_match_count, text_match_count, results}`; `content[0].text` is a human-readable summary like `"12 results (4 symbol, 10 text, 2 merged)"`. `isError: false` for every non-error path — empty result set is a success.

Dedicated response/error helpers mirroring the WO-0033 / WO-0034 module structure: a private `search_code_found_response(id, query, root, symbol_count, text_count, results_json) -> Value` builder, a private `read_symbol_matches(kg, query, limit) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, SearchCodeReadError>` wrapper around the KG call, and a private `merge_search_results(symbols: Vec<Entity>, texts: Vec<TextMatch>, max_results: usize) -> Vec<SearchCodeResult>` pure function with its own `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests covering (a) no overlap, (b) full overlap, (c) partial overlap, (d) `max_results` truncation after merge. `SearchCodeReadError` is `(code: i64, message: String)` — same pattern as `FindDefinitionReadError` / `GetConventionsReadError`. `SearchCodeResult` is a `serde::Serialize` struct; derive Debug+Clone+PartialEq.

Add the acceptance test `test_search_code_basic` as a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` item in `server.rs`, placed adjacent to `test_get_conventions_tool`, so the nextest selector `server::test_search_code_basic` resolves per the WO-0006 test-selector rule and DEC-0007. Test body: (a) `tempfile::TempDir` as the project root; write `src/util.rs` containing `pub fn banana_split(x: u32) -> u32 { x + 1 }\n` so the text scan AND the KG symbol match both trigger on `"banana_split"`; (b) open a separate KG via `KnowledgeGraph::open` on a second temp path, insert a matching `Entity` via `upsert_entity` with `name = "banana_split"`, `qualified_name = "project::util::banana_split"`, `file_path = <abs path to util.rs>`, `start_line = 1`, `signature = Some("fn banana_split(x: u32) -> u32")`; (c) wrap KG in `Arc::new(Mutex::new(kg))`; (d) build `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg)`; (e) invoke `server.handle_line(r#"{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":1,\"method\":\"tools/call\",\"params\":{\"name\":\"search_code\",\"arguments\":{\"query\":\"banana_split\",\"root\":\"<tempdir path>\",\"max_results\":50}}}}"#)` (with the tempdir path substituted at runtime); (f) assert `_meta.count >= 1`, `_meta.symbol_match_count == 1`, `_meta.text_match_count >= 1`, and that at least one result has `source == "both"` (the symbol row and the text row at `util.rs:1` merge); (g) assert `_meta.results[0].preview` contains the `banana_split` string; (h) every assertion uses real data — no mocks of SQLite, `ignore`, or `grep-searcher`.

Supplementary `#[cfg(test)]` tests adjacent to the main acceptance test: (i) `test_search_code_tool_empty_query` — assert empty `arguments.query` → JSON-RPC `error.code == -32602`; (ii) `test_search_code_tool_no_kg_returns_stub` — `McpServer::new()` (no KG attached), assert the response returns `_meta.not_yet_implemented == true` (phase-1 invariant #9); (iii) `test_search_code_tool_non_string_query` — `arguments.query: 42`, assert `error.code == -32602`; (iv) `test_search_code_tool_nonexistent_root` — `arguments.root = "/this/path/does/not/exist"`, assert `error.code == -32602`; (v) `test_search_code_tool_max_results_clamp` — set `max_results = 10000`, assert the handler clamps to 500 and a `tracing::warn` is emitted (use `tracing-subscriber` test fixture or just assert the cap at the result-count level); (vi) `test_search_code_tool_only_text_no_symbol` — call with a query that matches the text file contents but has no matching KG entity, assert `_meta.symbol_match_count == 0` and `_meta.text_match_count >= 1` and every result has `source == "text"`.

Rustdoc on every new public / `pub(crate)` item (`search_entities_by_name`, `TextMatch`, `TextSearchError`, `text_search`, `SearchCodeResult`, `handle_search_code`, `search_code_found_response`, `read_symbol_matches`, `merge_search_results`, `SEARCH_CODE_MAX_RESULTS`) referencing: master-plan §3.2 row 4 (`search_code` — hybrid search), master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 5 line 1765 (Phase 1 deliverable), master-plan §12.1 (entities schema), phase-1 CLAUDE.md invariant #9 (other 19 tools keep the stub after this WO), DEC-0009 (in-process ripgrep rationale), and feature-list:P1-W5-F09 frozen selector `-p ucil-daemon server::test_search_code_basic`.

Errors surfaced via the existing `KnowledgeGraphError` enum (no new variant needed — the generic `Sqlite` variant covers statement failures). New `TextSearchError` enum in `text_search.rs` with `thiserror::Error`. No new error type in the `McpServer` public surface; reuse `jsonrpc_error` for -32602 / -32603 envelopes.

`ready-for-review.md` at `ucil-build/work-orders/0035-ready-for-review.md` summarising the diff, commits, acceptance outcome, and a stash-based reality-check evidence block per the WO-0033 / WO-0034 precedent.
scope_out: Full G1 (Structural) / G2 (Search) fusion as called out in master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1783 (`Wire into find_definition, find_references, search_code`). This WO delivers the Phase-1 foundational handler (symbol index + ripgrep text); Serena + ast-grep + LSP enrichment is a Phase-2 concern and will layer on via a new dispatch branch without rewriting this one.
Semantic / vector search (master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8). LanceDB embedding integration is explicitly Phase 2 scope.
Warm-tier / cold-tier cross-session dedup (master-plan §6.3 session dedup, line 666). This WO does NOT consult `session_manager::SessionState::files_in_context`; that layer arrives in Phase 2.

CEQP universal-parameter semantic processing (`reason`, `task_goal`, `files_in_context`, `non_obvious_info_agent_has`). The schema advertises them (P1-W4-F06 passing); this WO reads them from `arguments` but does NOT act on them. Applying `reason` for enrichment is a Phase-2+ concern.

Handler wiring for the remaining 19 MCP tools still stubbed after WO-0033/0034 (`understand_code` = P1-W4-F09, `find_similar`, `get_context_for_edit`, `get_architecture`, `trace_dependencies`, `blast_radius`, `explain_history`, `remember`, `review_changes`, `check_quality`, `run_tests`, `security_scan`, `lint_code`, `type_check`, `refactor`, `generate_docs`, `query_database`, `check_runtime`, `find_references`) — each is a separate feature with its own WO (or already shipped per WO-0011/etc., not tracked here).
File-watcher-driven incremental re-indexing on edit. This WO reads from the KG as it exists at call time; re-indexing is the file-watcher's job (P1-W3-F02 — already passing).
Search-history / query-telemetry persistence. The handler emits `tracing` spans but does NOT write to the KG / metrics store.
Changes to any file under `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`, `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json`, or the master plan.
Changes to the 22-tool catalog or its `ucil_tools()` builder — the catalog is frozen under WO-0010's acceptance test (`server::test_all_22_tools_registered` must stay green).
Changes to `McpServer::serve` control flow beyond the single new dispatch branch in `handle_tools_call`.
Changes to the existing `find_definition` / `get_conventions` handlers, their helpers, or their tests.
acceptance: `search_code` MCP tool returns merged results from (a) the KG `search_entities_by_name` read helper and (b) an in-process `text_search` walker + matcher (via `ignore` + `grep-searcher` + `grep-regex`) driven by a real `KnowledgeGraph::open` + `upsert_entity` sequence on a temp SQLite and real file contents in a `tempfile::TempDir`. De-duplication by `(file_path, line_number)` produces the merged `source: "both"` record when both sources hit the same line.
Empty result path returns `_meta.count == 0`, `_meta.results == []`, and `isError == false` — no results is a successful empty response.
The frozen acceptance selector `server::test_search_code_basic` resolves under `cargo nextest` with the mainline executor profile per DEC-0007 and the WO-0006 test-selector rule.
The 22-tool catalog stays wire-compatible: `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` stays green and every still-stubbed tool keeps returning `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`.
Regression safety on prior handlers: `server::test_find_definition_tool` and `server::test_get_conventions_tool` stay green — the new dispatch branch does not shadow or alter the existing gating.
No regressions across the workspace: `cargo nextest run --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` stay green. `cargo fmt --all --check` is clean.

Zero new stubs introduced: `grep -nE 'todo!\\(|unimplemented!\\(|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError'` across the WO diff yields zero hits. Phase-1 invariant #9 preserved for the 19 tools that still stub.
Every public / `pub(crate)` item added has rustdoc; `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` and `cargo doc -p ucil-core --no-deps` emit zero warnings/errors.
`.gitignore` respect is proven by the `text_search.rs` unit test (file under an ignored path is NOT returned).

Commit cadence per CLAUDE.md ≤50-LOC-per-commit target: (1) workspace-deps add + DEC-0009 reference + ADR link commented alongside each new dep; (2) `KnowledgeGraph::search_entities_by_name` + rustdoc; (3) KG unit tests for the read helper; (4) `text_search.rs` skeleton (`TextMatch`, `TextSearchError`); (5) `text_search` implementation (`ignore` + `grep-searcher` wiring); (6) `text_search.rs` unit test (`.gitignore` + `max_results` + line numbers); (7) `SearchCodeResult` + `merge_search_results` + its unit tests; (8) `handle_search_code` happy path + dispatch wiring; (9) error paths (empty query → -32602, non-string query → -32602, nonexistent root → -32602, max_results clamp); (10) `test_search_code_basic` + five negative tests; (11) rustdoc polish + `ready-for-review.md`. 10-12 commits total. Each commit pushed immediately per CLAUDE.md.

`reality-check.sh` (verifier step 6b) passes: stashing the `handle_search_code` method + dispatch wiring causes `test_search_code_basic` to FAIL; popping restores GREEN. Proves the test exercises real code, not a mock.
`ready-for-review.md` exists at `ucil-build/work-orders/0035-ready-for-review.md` and summarises the implementation + acceptance outcome.
